product_overview: A headless CMS backend that exposes a physical DDL engine: admins design database schemas which create real SQL tables and immediately expose CRUD APIs per table. The system includes JWT auth, role-based guards, QR login, import tools (SQL/CSV/XLSX), group-based permissions and public read-only APIs.
core_goals: Allow admins to define physical database schemas (tables/columns/foreign keys) and automatically expose tenant‑isolated CRUD endpoints.
Provide a secure, role-based access model (master / admin / moderator) with QR login and JWT authentication.
Enable safe imports of SQL dumps and CSV/XLSX data with dry-run preview and tenant metadata registration.
Support grouping of tables and granting moderator permissions at the group level, with support for public read-only tables.

features.name: Authentication
features.description: Login via username/password (OAuth2PasswordRequestForm), QR login flow for device pairing, and JWT tokens for authenticated requests.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with form data username/password -> Receive 200 with { access_token, token_type, user } (happy path)
POST /api/auth/login with form data username/wrong_password -> Receive 401 Invalid credentials (error path)
POST /api/auth/qr/session (no auth) -> Receive 200 with { session_id, expires_at } (create session)
POST /api/auth/qr/session -> Wait >5 minutes -> GET /api/auth/qr/status/{session_id} -> Receive 400 Session expired (error: expired session)

POST /api/auth/qr/session -> Receive session_id -> Authenticated user: POST /api/auth/qr/authorize with Authorization: Bearer <token> and body { session_id } -> Receive 200 { message } -> Polling client: GET /api/auth/qr/status/{session_id} -> Receive 200 { is_authorized: true, access_token: string, user: User } (happy QR flow)
POST /api/auth/qr/authorize with invalid/unknown session_id and Authorization: Bearer <token> -> Receive 404 Session not found (error path)

features.name: Master - Admin Management
features.description: Master users create, list and delete admin accounts and list all non-master users.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with master form data -> Receive 200 with access_token (authenticate as master)
POST /api/admins with Authorization: Bearer <master_token> and body { username, password } -> Receive 200 UserResponse (create admin happy path)
GET /api/admins with Authorization: Bearer <master_token> -> Receive 200 UserResponse[] (list admins)
DELETE /api/admins/{admin_id} with Authorization: Bearer <master_token> -> Receive 200 { message } (delete admin happy path)
POST /api/admins with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> and body { username, password } -> Receive 403 Not master role (error: non-master cannot create admin)
GET /api/all-users with Authorization: Bearer <master_token> -> Receive 200 UserResponse[] (list all non-master users happy path)

features.name: Admin - Moderator Management
features.description: Admins create and manage their own moderators, including password resets and deletion of moderators they own.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with admin form data -> Receive 200 with access_token (authenticate as admin)
POST /api/moderators with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> and body { username, password } -> Receive 200 UserResponse (create moderator happy path)
GET /api/moderators with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> -> Receive 200 UserResponse[] (list own moderators)
POST /api/moderators/{mod_id}/reset-password with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> and body { new_password } -> Receive 200 { message } (reset password happy path)
DELETE /api/moderators/{mod_id} with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> -> Receive 200 { message } (delete own moderator happy path)
POST /api/moderators with Authorization: Bearer <moderator_token> and body { username, password } -> Receive 403 Not admin role (error: non-admin cannot create moderator)
DELETE /api/moderators/{mod_id} with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> for a moderator not owned by this admin -> Receive 403 Not your moderator or 404 Moderator not found (error path)

features.name: Database Groups and Permissions
features.description: Admins create named groups of tables and grant or revoke moderator access to those groups.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with admin form data -> Receive 200 with access_token (authenticate as admin)
POST /api/database-groups with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> and body { name, description } -> Receive 200 DatabaseGroupResponse (create group happy path)
GET /api/database-groups with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> -> Receive 200 DatabaseGroupResponse[] (list accessible groups)
POST /api/database-groups/{group_id}/permissions with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> and body { moderator_id } -> Receive 200 PermissionResponse (grant moderator access happy path)
DELETE /api/database-groups/{group_id}/permissions/{mod_id} with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> -> Receive 200 { message } (revoke permission happy path)
POST /api/database-groups with Authorization: Bearer <master_token> and body { name, description } -> Receive 403 Master cannot own groups (error path)

POST /api/database-groups/{group_id}/permissions with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> and body { moderator_id } for non-existent moderator or group -> Receive 404 Group or moderator not found (error path)

features.name: Dynamic Table Engine
features.description: Admins define tables (columns, types, FK references) and the backend executes DDL to create tenant-prefixed physical tables and metadata records.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with admin form data -> Receive 200 with access_token (authenticate as admin)
POST /tables/ with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> and body { name, description, group_id, is_public, columns: [...] } -> Receive 200 TableResponse (create table happy path)
GET /tables/ with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> -> Receive 200 TableResponse[] including new table and columns (list tables happy path)
POST /tables/ with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> with body for a table name that already exists under the tenant -> Receive 400 Table already exists or DDL error (error path)
POST /tables/ with Authorization: Bearer <master_token> and body { ... } -> Receive 403 Master cannot create tables (error path)
PATCH /tables/{table_id}/visibility with Authorization: Bearer <table_owner_token> -> Receive 200 { is_public: boolean } (toggle visibility happy path)
PATCH /tables/{table_id}/visibility with Authorization: Bearer <non_owner_token> -> Receive 403 Not your table or 404 Table not found (error path)

features.name: Relations API
features.description: Create and query logical FK relation records used by the UI to render relationships between dynamic tables.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with admin form data -> Receive 200 with access_token (authenticate as admin)

POST /api/relations with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> and body { name, from_table_id, to_table_id, relation_type, from_column_name, to_column_name } -> Receive 200 RelationResponse (create relation happy path)
GET /api/relations/table/{table_name} with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> -> Receive 200 RelationInfo[] (list relations for table happy path)
DELETE /api/relations/{relation_id} with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> -> Receive 200 { message } (delete relation happy path)
POST /api/relations with Authorization: Bearer <moderator_token> and body { ... } -> Receive 403 Admin required (error path)
GET /api/relations/table/{nonexistent_table} with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> -> Receive 404 Table not found (error path)

features.name: Dynamic CRUD
features.description: Authenticated CRUD operations on tenant‑scoped dynamic tables exposed at /api/{table_name}, honoring group permissions and tenant isolation.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with moderator form data -> Receive 200 with access_token (authenticate as moderator)
POST /api/{table_name} with Authorization: Bearer <moderator_token> and JSON body { column1: value, ... } -> Receive 200 { message: string, id: integer } (insert record happy path)
GET /api/{table_name} with Authorization: Bearer <moderator_token> -> Receive 200 record[] (list records happy path)
PUT /api/{table_name}/{record_id} with Authorization: Bearer <moderator_token> and JSON body { column1: new_value } -> Receive 200 { message: string } (update record happy path)
DELETE /api/{table_name}/{record_id} with Authorization: Bearer <moderator_token> -> Receive 200 { message: string } (delete record happy path)
GET /api/{table_name} with Authorization: Bearer <moderator_token> for a table outside moderator's permitted groups -> Receive 403 or 404 Table not found or no access (error path)
PUT /api/{table_name}/{nonexistent_id} with Authorization: Bearer <moderator_token> and JSON body { ... } -> Receive 404 Record or table not found / no access (error path)

features.name: Public API
features.description: Read-only endpoints for tables marked public; supports filtering, sorting, pagination and column metadata without authentication.
user_flows: GET /public/tables/ (no auth) -> Receive 200 { id, name, description, columns }[] (list public tables happy path)

GET /public/api/{table_name}?filter_col=col&filter_val=val&filter_op=eq&sort=col&order=asc&limit=10&offset=0 (no auth) -> Receive 200 { data: record[], total, limit, offset } (query public table happy path)
GET /public/api/{table_name}/columns (no auth) -> Receive 200 ColumnResponse[] (get public table columns happy path)
GET /public/relations/ (no auth) -> Receive 200 { id, name, from_table, to_table, relation_type }[] (list public relations happy path)
GET /public/api/{non_public_table} (no auth) -> Receive 404 Table not found or not public (error path)

features.name: SQL Script Import
features.description: Admins may dry-run and commit .sql imports; the dry-run filters/flags disallowed statements (DROP/ALTER/DELETE/TRUNCATE) and reports conflicts before executing.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with admin form data -> Receive 200 with access_token (authenticate as admin)
POST /api/import/sql/dry-run with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> and .sql multipart upload -> Receive 200 { summary: { total, ok, blocked, conflicts }, statements: [...] } (dry-run happy path)

POST /api/import/sql with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> and .sql multipart upload after a successful dry-run -> Receive 200 { created_tables: string[], inserted_rows: integer, errors: string[] } (commit import happy path)

POST /api/import/sql/dry-run with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> and .sql that contains DROP DATABASE -> Receive 200 with statements including status: blocked and summary.blocked>0 (dry-run blocks hazardous statements error path)
POST /api/import/sql with Authorization: Bearer <master_token> and .sql file -> Receive 403 Master cannot import (error path)

features.name: CSV and XLSX Data Import
features.description: Admins import CSV/XLSX into existing dynamic tables by header matching; unmatched columns are ignored and result includes inserted_rows and errors.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with admin form data -> Receive 200 with access_token (authenticate as admin)

POST /api/import/data/{table_name} with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> and multipart .csv file -> Receive 200 { inserted_rows: integer, total_rows: integer, matched_columns: [...], errors: [...] } (CSV import happy path)

POST /api/import/data/{table_name} with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> and multipart .xlsx file -> Receive 200 { inserted_rows, total_rows, matched_columns, errors } (XLSX import happy path when openpyxl present)

POST /api/import/data/{table_name} with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> and file with no matching columns or unsupported type -> Receive 400 No matching columns or unsupported file type (error path)
POST /api/import/data/{table_name} with Authorization: Bearer <master_token> and .csv -> Receive 403 Master cannot import (error path)
POST /api/import/data/{nonexistent_table} with Authorization: Bearer <admin_token> and .csv -> Receive 404 Table not found or no access (error path)

features.name: Authentication
features.description: JWT-based login, QR code login flow, and role-based access guards

endpoints.description: Login with username and password, returns JWT access token

response_schema.200: { access_token: string, token_type: string, user: { id, username, role } }

endpoints.description: Create a new QR login session, valid for 5 minutes
Poll QR session status; returns JWT if authorized

response_schema.200: { is_authorized: bool, access_token?: string, user?: User }

endpoints.description: Authorize a QR session (called by authenticated user on mobile)

features.name: Master - Admin Management
features.description: Master account manages admin users

endpoints.description: Create a new admin user (master only)
List all admin users (master only)
Delete an admin user (master only)
List all non-master users (master only)

features.name: Admin - Moderator Management
features.description: Admin users manage their own moderators

endpoints.description: Create a moderator under the authenticated admin
List moderators owned by authenticated admin
Delete a moderator (must be owned by admin)
Reset a moderator's password

features.name: Database Groups and Permissions
features.description: Admins create groups of tables; moderators are granted group-level access

endpoints.description: Create a database group (admin only)
List groups accessible to the authenticated user
Delete a database group
Grant a moderator access to a group
Revoke a moderator's access to a group

features.name: Dynamic Table Engine
features.description: Create physical database tables dynamically with DDL engine; supports FK constraints

endpoints.description: Create a new physical table with columns; supports FK references via fk_table/fk_column

response_schema.400: Table already exists or DDL error

endpoints.description: List all tables accessible to the authenticated user (includes columns)
Toggle a table between public and private

features.name: Relations API
features.description: Manage FK relations between dynamic tables for UI rendering

endpoints.description: Create a logical FK relation record (admin only)
Get all FK relations where the given table is the source
Delete a relation record

features.name: Dynamic CRUD
features.description: Full CRUD on any dynamic table; tenant-isolated by admin ownership

endpoints.description: List all records in a dynamic table

response_schema.404: Table not found or no access

endpoints.description: Insert a new record into a dynamic table

response_schema.404: Table not found or no access

endpoints.description: Update a record by ID; tenant-isolated

response_schema.404: Record or table not found / no access

endpoints.description: Delete a record by ID; tenant-isolated

response_schema.404: Record or table not found / no access

features.name: Public API
features.description: Read-only access to tables marked as public; no authentication required

endpoints.description: List all public tables with their column definitions
Get records from a public table with filtering, sorting, and pagination

response_schema.200: { data: record[], total: integer, limit: integer, offset: integer }
response_schema.404: Table not found or not public

endpoints.description: Get column definitions for a public table

response_schema.404: Table not found or not public

endpoints.description: List FK relations between public tables

features.name: SQL Script Import
features.description: Import .sql dumps with safety filtering; supports dry-run preview before commit

endpoints.description: Parse and validate a .sql file without executing; returns per-statement report

response_schema.200: { summary: { total, ok, blocked, conflicts }, statements: [{ type, status, message, table_name }] }

endpoints.description: Execute a validated .sql import; atomically registers _tables and _columns

response_schema.200: { created_tables: string[], inserted_rows: integer, errors: string[] }

features.name: CSV and XLSX Data Import
features.description: Import data files into existing dynamic tables by matching column headers

endpoints.description: Upload CSV or XLSX file; columns matched by name, unmatched ignored

response_schema.200: { inserted_rows: integer, total_rows: integer, matched_columns: string[], errors: string[] }
response_schema.400: No matching columns or unsupported file type
response_schema.404: Table not found or no access

known_limitations.issue: ForeignKeyConstraint only applied at table creation time; cannot add FK to existing tables
known_limitations.impact: SQLite does not support ALTER TABLE ADD CONSTRAINT; FK is a creation-time-only feature

known_limitations.issue: Route /api/{table_name} may shadow tables named 'admins' or 'moderators'
known_limitations.impact: FastAPI resolves literal routes first, so this is low risk but is a design smell

known_limitations.issue: CRUD endpoints for dynamic tables do not support bulk insert or batch delete
